findings.message: The final count of 5 qualifying appearances is not justified by the supplied text. Only the 107-109s sequence is explicitly checked for co-visibility of salad/lettuce and a cutting board, while the claimed qualifying intervals at 50-59s, 124-126s, 256-258s, and 270-273s are asserted without textual evidence that a cutting board is present there.

The trace treats visibility candidate intervals as if they were validated qualifying appearances with a cutting board. Candidate coverage for lettuce/salad visibility does not by itself establish the answer-critical cutting-board condition.

The exclusion of 261-263s as 'not an additional distinct appearance' is unsupported because the trace never textually establishes whether 256-258s, 261-263s, and 270-273s are the same continuous shot, separate shots, or whether any of them include a cutting board.

feedback: The trace correctly identifies salad as the insulted ingredient, but the count is under-grounded. It must textually validate the cutting-board condition for each counted interval and justify the distinct-appearance grouping before selecting option C.
missing_information: for each counted salad/lettuce interval, whether a cutting board is visible in the same shot
which distinct qualifying appearances are grounded after checking all salad/lettuce candidate intervals
whether 256-258s, 261-263s, and 270-273s are separate qualifying appearances or part of the same appearance

diagnostics.referent_alignment: pass: the trace textually grounds the insulted ingredient as salad from transcript evidence.
diagnostics.temporal_alignment: partial: candidate salad/lettuce intervals are listed, but most counted intervals lack answer-critical visual validation.
diagnostics.option_alignment: fail: option C is selected from an unsupported total of 5.
diagnostics.evidence_sufficiency: fail: supplied text supports at most one explicit qualifying appearance around 107-109s and two explicit non-qualifying sampled cases; the remaining counted appearances are not textually grounded.
